---
description: "Inbox stage rules for planning workspace intake."
applyTo: "docs/00-09-admin/03-planning-workspace/inbox/**/*.md"
---

# planning workspace — inbox

## purpose

Capture raw inputs (requests, ideas, observations, incidents) quickly with minimal structure, ensuring nothing is lost before triage.

## entry criteria

Work belongs in inbox when:

- it is new, untriaged, or unclear
- scope, priority, or owner is not yet determined
- it may require clarification or decomposition

## allowed operations

- record the input with enough context to be actionable later
- attach links, references, screenshots, or source notes
- ask clarifying questions and add missing context
- propose a candidate category, repo, or owner (tentative)

## exit criteria

Move an item out of inbox when:

- the request is understood well enough for triage
- you can identify likely scope, impact, and next steps

Typical exits:

- inbox → backlog (most common)
- inbox → archive (invalid/duplicate/no longer relevant, with reason)

## required artifacts

Minimum content for an inbox item:

- short title (what is being requested)
- source (who/where it came from)
- brief context (why it matters)
- initial unknowns (what must be clarified)

## handoff expectations

When moving to backlog:

- add a first-pass scope statement
- add risks/constraints if obvious
- suggest a priority and owner (if possible)

## guardrails

- do not start implementation from inbox
- do not make binding decisions here (record options only)
- avoid long narratives; keep capture concise

---
